mnemonics.title: Electromagnetic Spectrum Order
mnemonics.content: RAGING MICE VISIBLY UTTERLY X-RAY GIRLS - Radio, AM, Gamma, Infrared, Microwave, Cellular, Infrared, Visible, UV, TV, Ultra, X-ray, Gamma (decreasing wavelength)

mnemonics.title: Modulation Types
mnemonics.content: AMP - Amplitude Modulates Portion (AM varies Amplitude, FM varies Frequency, PM varies Phase)

mnemonics.title: Propagation Types
mnemonics.content: GSS - Ground, Sky, Space. Ground follows Earth, Sky uses ionosphere, Space goes straight

acronyms.usage: Varies carrier amplitude; used in AM radio broadcasting (540-1600 kHz)
Varies carrier frequency; used in FM radio (88-108 MHz), provides better sound quality than AM
Varies carrier phase; used in digital communications; closely related to FM
Unlicensed frequency bands (2.4 GHz, 5 GHz) used for Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, microwaves
Direct path between transmitter and receiver without obstructions; required for microwave links
Measure of signal quality; higher SNR means better communication quality
Technique used in Bluetooth where frequency changes rapidly to avoid interference
Technique used in Wi-Fi 802.11b where data is spread across wide bandwidth
Electromagnetic frequencies used for wireless communication, typically 3 kHz to 300 GHz
Unit for measuring antenna gain relative to a theoretical isotropic radiator

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a rainbow as a ladder. Red (long wavelength, low frequency) at bottom, Violet (short wavelength, high frequency) at top. Each 'rung' represents a different type of wave with unique properties and uses.

Think of three messengers: (1) Ground walker - follows Earth's surface, good around obstacles. (2) Sky jumper - bounces off ionosphere, goes over horizon. (3) Sprinter - runs in straight line, fastest but blocked by obstacles.

Think of the carrier wave as a truck, and the information (voice/data) as packages. Modulation is loading packages onto the truck - you can stack them (AM), change the truck's speed (FM), or rearrange their order (PM).

Imagine a lightbulb (isotropic) glowing equally in all directions. A reflector (directional antenna) concentrates light in one direction - it's brighter in that direction but dimmer elsewhere. That's gain!

analogies.analogy: Bandwidth is like road width - a 4-lane highway carries more cars than a 1-lane road. Data rate is like traffic flow - how many cars pass per second. More lanes (bandwidth) = more traffic (data).

Think of ocean waves crashing on a beach. High frequency = waves coming rapidly (many per minute). Wavelength = distance between consecutive waves. They're inversely related - more frequent waves are closer together.

analogies.analization: It's like sound getting quieter as you move away from a speaker. At double the distance, you hear only 1/4 as loud (inverse square law). Same happens to radio signals traveling through space.

analogies.analogy: Imagine seeing multiple echoes when shouting in a hall with pillars. The original sound and reflections arrive at different times, sometimes canceling (destructive) or adding (constructive). That's fading in wireless!

Sending a letter (information) in an envelope (carrier). Without envelope, the letter is fragile and easily damaged. The carrier (envelope) protects and carries your message to destination where it's extracted (demodulated).
